title: Tender Snappy Sicilian Shrimp Focaccia
description: A salty and snappy Italian dish featuring shrimp, orange juice, and a romantic Sicilian twist, perfect for a special occasion, made without onion or garlic.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the shrimp for 2-3 minutes, or until they turn pink.
Immediately transfer the cooked shrimp to an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
In a large bowl, whisk together the orange juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the cooked shrimp to the bowl and toss to coat with the orange juice mixture.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the focaccia bread in half horizontally and place on a baking sheet.
Spread the shrimp mixture evenly over the bottom half of the focaccia bread.
Sprinkle the lemon zest, parsley, and mozzarella cheese over the top of the shrimp mixture.
Place the top half of the focaccia bread on the baking sheet and bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the bread is toasted.
Remove the focaccia from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
